title: Design, Development, Implementation, Testing and Commissioning of a Customer Relationship Management System (CRM) for BRS Kenya (BRS/OT/005/2025/2026). This is a comprehensive procurement for a centralized CRM system to manage business registration and customer engagement in Nairobi, Kenya. The tender encompasses system design, data migration, and technical support for a multi-year term beginning in the 2025/2026 fiscal year. Bidders must adhere to regulatory compliance under the Business Registration Service (BRS) framework and provide proof of previous similar implementations within East Africa. The procurement involves full software lifecycle management and integration with existing state databases for streamlined operations and service delivery for government entities and the public. Tenderers are required to submit their bids through the official e-procurement portal and include mandatory bid documentation as specified in the solicitation notice issued in late May 2026. The estimated duration for implementation and initial testing is six months from the date of contract award, with a maintenance phase spanning two years post-deployment. Clarifications are to be sought through the BRS procurement office five days before closing as per local regulatory standards for ICT projects in Kenya. Final bids must be submitted electronically and physically where indicated in the tender document provided on the Tender Yetu and PPIP platforms for public transparency in procurement activities across the Kenyan government sectors and related state agencies under the Ministry of Information, Communications and the Digital Economy for enhanced digital transformation goals across the country and the region for the 2026 fiscal cycle as planned in the development agenda for Kenyan digital identity and data management infrastructure projects. The contract is intended to enhance the efficiency of business registration processes and provide a robust data management solution for the national registration authority to support its growing
